devanagari: उद्धवमंत्रणया श्रीकृष्णस्येन्द्रप्रस्थगमनम् । श्रीशुक उवाच । इत्युदीरितमाकर्ण्य देवऋषेरुद्धवोऽब्रवीत् । सभ्यानां मतमाज्ञाय कृष्णस्य च म...
english_devnagari: uddhavamaṃtraṇayā śrīkṛṣṇasyendraprasthagamanam śrīśuka uvāca ityudīritamākarṇya devaṛṣeruddhavo sabhyānāṃ matamājñāya kṛṣṇasya ca mahāmatiḥ

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Having heard the words of the divine sage Nārada, and considering the views of the members of the assembly and that of Lord Kṛṣṇa, the greatly intelligent Uddhava spoke as follows:

devanagari: श्रीउद्धव उवाच । यदुक्तं ऋषिणा देव साचिव्यं यक्ष्यतस्त्वया । कार्यं पैतृष्वसेयस्य रक्षा च शरणैषिणाम् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīuddhava uvāca yaduktaṃ ṛṣiṇā deva sācivyaṃ yakṣyatastvayā kāryaṃ paitṛṣvaseyasya rakṣā ca śaraṇaiṣiṇām

translation:
Uddhava said:
O Lord! As advised by the sage Nārada, you should render help to your cousins (sons of your paternal aunt) in the performance of that sacrifice and also should extend protection to those (kings in the captivity of Jarāsandha) who desire to seek your shelter.

devanagari: यष्टव्यम्राजसूयेन दिक् चक्रजयिना विभो । अतो जरासुतजय उभयार्थो मतो मम ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: yaṣṭavyamrājasūyena dik cakrajayinā vibho ato jarāsutajaya ubhayārtho mato mama

translation:
O Almighty Lord! It is only the conquerer of all the cardinal points who is eligible to perform the Ṛā jasūya sacrifice.
[Bhāgavata Candrikā’s Text adds:]
3(A). We should at first set out from this place to go to Indraprastha. It is there (at Indraprastha) that with Yudhiṣṭhira’s permission, you should slay Jarāsandha for the sake of Yudhiṣṭhira. Hence, in my opinion, victory over the son of Jarā (i.e. Jarāsandha) will serve both the purposes.

devanagari: अस्माकं च महानर्थो ह्येतेनैव भविष्यति । यशश्च तव गोविन्द राज्ञो बद्धान् विमुञ्चतः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: asmākaṃ ca mahānartho hyetenaiva bhaviṣyati yaśaśca tava govinda rājño baddhān vimuñcataḥ

translation: We shall be achieving our great object by this act (of vanquishing Jarāsandha). And great glory will accrue to you by setting at liberty those imprisoned kings, O Govinda!

devanagari: स वै दुर्विषहो राजा नागायुतसमो बले । बलिनामपि चान्येषां भीमं समबलं विना ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: sa vai durviṣaho rājā nāgāyutasamo bale balināmapi cānyeṣāṃ bhīmaṃ samabalaṃ vinā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[imperfect active second plural], [imperfect middle third single]

translation: (To Yādavas who are eager to kill Jarāsandha, Uddhava cautions:) In physical strength that king (Jarāsandha) equals ten thousand elephants and as such, is irresistible to others who (regard themselves and actually) are mighty, except Bhīma who is his equal in strength.

devanagari: द्वैरथे स तु जेतव्यो मा शताक्षौहिणीयुतः । ब्रह्मण्योऽभ्यर्थितो विप्रैः न प्रत्याख्याति कर्हिचित् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: dvairathe sa tu jetavyo mā śatākṣauhiṇīyutaḥ brahmaṇyo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ ji class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ ji class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ yu class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ yu class 6 verb], [nominative single from √ yu class 9 verb]

translation: It is only in a single combat that he can be conquered and not otherwise (with a powerful army), as he commands an army of one hundred akṣauhiṇīs. He is friendly to Brāhmaṇas. And when requested by Brāhmaṇas, he would never refuse it.

devanagari: ब्रह्मवेषधरो गत्वा तं भिक्षेत वृकोदरः । हनिष्यति न सन्देहो द्वैरथे तव सन्निधौ ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: brahmaveṣadharo gatvā taṃ bhikṣeta vṛkodaraḥ haniṣyati na sandeho dvairathe tava sannidhau

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [optative active second plural], [optative middle third single]
[locative single from √ han class 1 verb], [locative single from √ han class 2 verb]
[locative single from √ han class 1 verb], [locative single from √ han class 2 verb]

translation: Let Bhīmasena assume the guise of a Brāhmaṇa and approach him to beg of him single-handed combat with himself. There is not the slightest doubt that in your presence Bhīma will certainly kill him in that duel.

devanagari: निमित्तं परमीशस्य विश्वसर्गनिरोधयोः । हिरण्यगर्भः शर्वश्च कालस्यारूपिणस्तव ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: nimittaṃ paramīśasya viśvasarganirodhayoḥ hiraṇyagarbhaḥ śarvaśca kālasyārūpiṇastava
translation: You are the formless Time-spirit who, as the Supreme Lord, are the real cause of the creation and destruction of the universe, while gods Brahmā and Rudra are mere instruments. So Bhīma will be the mere instrumental cause of killing Jarāsandha while (as a matter of fact) you are the real slayer.

devanagari: गायन्ति ते विशदकर्म गृहेषु देव्यो । राज्ञां स्वशत्रुवधमात्मविमोक्षणं च । गोप्यश्च कुञ्जरपतेर्जनकात्मजायाः । पित्रोश्च लब्धशरणा मुनयो वय...
english_devnagari: gāyanti te viśadakarma gṛheṣu devyo rājñāṃ svaśatruvadhamātmavimokṣaṇaṃ ca gopyaśca kuñjarapaterjanakātmajāyāḥ pitrośca labdhaśaraṇā munayo vayaṃ ca
translation: (When Jarāsandha will be killed) the queens of those (released) kings will sing in their (respective) palaces your glorious act in slaying their own (common) enemy (who imprisoned their husbands) and of setting at liberty their husbands (who are dear to them as their own selves), just as Gopīs sing of their deliverance (from the demon Śaṅkhacūḍa;) sages who resort to you for shelter glorify you for the rescue of the king of elephants (from the alligator), and the release of Sītā, the daughter of Janaka (from Rāvaṇa’s captivity) and we who are protected by you, sing of your exploit in setting free your parents (Vasudeva and Devakī from the imprisonment of Kaṃsa).

devanagari: जरासन्धवधः कृष्ण भूर्यर्थायोपकल्पते । प्रायः पाकविपाकेन तव चाभिमतः क्रतुः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: jarāsandhavadhaḥ kṛṣṇa bhūryarthāyopakalpate prāyaḥ pākavipākena tava cābhimataḥ kratuḥ
translation: O Kṛṣṇa! The killing of Jarāsandha will tend to serve many great objectives (purposes) with the fruition of the meritorious acts of the kings (resulting in their release and re-instatement on their thrones), and the fructification of the evil acts of Jarāsandha (resulting in his death and that of Śiśupāla as well). For performance of the Rājasūya sacrifice meets your approval.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । इत्युद्धव वचो राजन् सर्वतोभद्रमच्युतम् । देवर्षिर्यदुवृद्धाश्च कृष्णश्च प्रत्यपूजयन् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca ityuddhava vaco rājan sarvatobhadramacyutam devarṣiryaduvṛddhāśca kṛṣṇaśca pratyapūjayan

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
O King! This counsel tendered by Uddhava was so correct and good in every respect that the divine sage Nārada, Yādava elders and Śrī Kṛṣṇa as well, expressed their approval of it.

devanagari: अथादिशत् प्रयाणाय भगवान् देवकीसुतः । भृत्यान् दारुकजैत्रादीन् अनुज्ञाप्य गुरून् विभुः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: athādiśat prayāṇāya bhagavān devakīsutaḥ bhṛtyān dārukajaitrādīn anujñāpya gurūn vibhuḥ
translation: Thereupon the all-pervading glorious Lord Kṛṣṇa, the son of Devakī, sought the permission of his elders and preceptors and ordered his servants like Dāruka, Jaitra and others to prepare for the journey.

devanagari: निर्गमय्यावरोधान् स्वान् ससुतान् सपरिच्छदान् । सङ्कर्षणमनुज्ञाप्य यदुराजं च शत्रुहन् । सूतोपनीतं स्वरथं आरुहद् गरुडध्वजम् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: nirgamayyāvarodhān svān sasutān saparicchadān saṅkarṣaṇamanujñāpya yadurājaṃ ca śatruhan sūtopanītaṃ svarathaṃ āruhad garuḍadhvajam

[nominative single from √ svṛ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ svṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ svṛ class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ svar class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ svar class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ svar class 1 verb]

translation: Having sent in advance, the ladies of his harem, sons, attendants and their kit, and after getting the formal permission of his elder brother Balarāma and Ugrasena, the king of Yādavas, Śrī Kṛṣṇa mounted his chariot brought to him by his charioteer and distinguished with the flag bearing the emblem of Garuḍa, O chastiser of enemies.

devanagari: ततो रथद्विपभटसादिनायकैः । करालया परिवृत आत्मसेनया । मृदङ्ग भेर्यानक शङ्खगोमुखैः । प्रघोषघोषितककुभो निराक्रमत् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: tato rathadvipabhaṭasādināyakaiḥ karālayā parivṛta ātmasenayā mṛdaṅga bheryānaka śaṅkhagomukhaiḥ praghoṣaghoṣitakakubho nirākramat
translation: Thereupon, surrounded with his formidable army comprising of chariots, elephants, foot-soldiers and horsemen and led by their respective military leaders, Kṛṣṇa set out from Dvārakā filling all the quarters with the brilliant fanfare and tremendous sound of the beating of tabors, drums, kettledrums, conchs and horns or trumpets.

devanagari: नृवाजिकाञ्चन शिबिकाभिरच्युतं । सहात्मजाः पतिमनु सुव्रता ययुः । वरांबराभरणविलेपनस्रजः । सुसंवृता नृभिरसिचर्मपाणिभिः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: nṛvājikāñcana śibikābhiracyutaṃ sahātmajāḥ patimanu suvratā yayuḥ varāṃbarābharaṇavilepanasrajaḥ susaṃvṛtā nṛbhirasicarmapāṇibhiḥ
translation: The devoted and virtuous queens of Kṛṣṇa, all clad in excellent garments, adorned with precious ornaments, pigments and garlands and well-guarded by attendants armed with swords and shields—followed their husbands along with their sons in golden palanquins and vehicles borne by men and drawn by horses.

devanagari: नरोष्ट्रगोमहिषखराश्वतर्यनः । करेणुभिः परिजनवारयोषितः । स्वलङ्कृताः कटकुटिकम्बलाम्बराद् । उपस्करा ययुरधियुज्य सर्वतः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: naroṣṭragomahiṣakharāśvataryanaḥ kareṇubhiḥ parijanavārayoṣitaḥ svalaṅkṛtāḥ kaṭakuṭikambalāmbarād upaskarā yayuradhiyujya sarvataḥ
translation: Mounting on vehicles and litters drawn by men, and on camels, oxen, buffaloes, donkeys, mules, carts and elephants and packing and loading their portable shelters of thatched cottages and tents, woollen blankets, cloths and other articles of their kit, on both sides of the animals, the attendants (of Kṛṣṇa) and courtesans all well-adorned, travelled in his train.

devanagari: बलं बृहद्ध्वजपटछत्रचामरैः । वरायुधाभरणकिरीटवर्मभिः । दिवांशुभिस्तुमुलरवं बभौ रवेः । यथार्णवः क्षुभिततिमिङ्गिलोर्मिभिः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: balaṃ bṛhaddhvajapaṭachatracāmaraiḥ varāyudhābharaṇakirīṭavarmabhiḥ divāṃśubhistumularavaṃ babhau raveḥ yathārṇavaḥ kṣubhitatimiṅgilormibhiḥ

[vocative single from √ kṣubh class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ kṣubh class 4 verb], [vocative single from √ kṣubh class 5 verb], [vocative single from √ kṣubh class 9 verb]
[vocative single from √ kṣubh class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ kṣubh class 4 verb], [vocative single from √ kṣubh class 5 verb], [vocative single from √ kṣubh class 9 verb]

translation: Like unto the sea grown agitated with, roaring high waves and restless whales, the enormous army, dinning with loud noise and with its lofty flags, big banners, umbrellas, chowries, excellent weapons, ornaments, diadems (or helmets) and armours, was shining brilliantly under the bright rays of the sun.

devanagari: अथो मुनिर्यदुपतिना सभाजितः । प्रणम्य तं हृदि विदधद् विहायसा । निशम्य तद्व्यवसितमाहृतार्हणो । मुकुन्दसन्दर्शननिर्वृतेन्द्रियः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: atho muniryadupatinā sabhājitaḥ praṇamya taṃ hṛdi vidadhad vihāyasā niśamya tadvyavasitamāhṛtārhaṇo mukundasandarśananirvṛtendriyaḥ
translation: Thereupon, the sage Nārada who was worshipped by Kṛṣṇa, the Lord of Yādavas, felt all his hearts and senses deeply satisfied and happy at the sight of Mukunḍa. After listening to his decision and accepting the reception offered to him, he bowed to Kṛṣṇa and meditating upon him (and thus installing him in his heart) he left Dvārakā, soaring up in the sky,

devanagari: राजदूतमुवाचेदं भगवान् प्रीणयन् गिरा । मा भैष्ट दूत भद्रं वो घातयिष्यामि मागधम् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: rājadūtamuvācedaṃ bhagavān prīṇayan girā mā bhaiṣṭa dūta bhadraṃ vo ghātayiṣyāmi māgadham

translation: (When Nārada left) the Lord promised the messenger in a kindly tone: “O messenger! You need not be afraid. May good betide you! (I shall see to it that) Jarāsandha, the king of Magadhas, be killed.”

devanagari: इत्युक्तः प्रस्थितो दूतो यथावद् अवदन् नृपान् । तेऽपि सन्दर्शनं शौरेः प्रत्यैक्षन् यन्मुमुक्षवः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: ityuktaḥ prasthito dūto yathāvad avadan nṛpān te

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vac class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ vac class 3 verb]

[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Thus assured by the Lord, the emissary departed and reported in details to the kings all that happened. The kings longed to get their freedom restored and eagerly looked forward to Kṛṣṇa’s actual appearance.

devanagari: आनर्तसौवीरमरून् तीर्त्वा विनशनं हरिः । गिरीन् नदीरतीयाय पुरग्रामव्रजाकरान् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: ānartasauvīramarūn tīrtvā vinaśanaṃ hariḥ girīn nadīratīyāya puragrāmavrajākarān
translation: Having travelled through Anarta[1], Sauvīra,[2] and Marus[3] and also Vinaśana[4], he crossed over mountains and rivers and passed by towns, villages, cowherd-settlements and quarries.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Gujarat and part of Malwa—GDAMI, p. 7.

Footnote [2]:
N.L. De accepts Alberuni’s identification of Sauvīra with Multan and Jahrawar—Ibid, p. 183.

Footnote [3]:
Marwar, a part of Rajasthan—Ibid, p. 127.

Footnote [4]:
Near modern Sirsa in Kurukṣetra or Brahmāvarta—the land between the Sarasvatī and the Dṛṣadvatī—ṢGAMI, p. 49.

devanagari: ततो दृषद्वतीं तीर्त्वा मुकुन्दोऽथ सरस्वतीम् । पञ्चालानथ मत्स्यांश्च शक्रप्रस्थमथागमत् ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: tato dṛṣadvatīṃ tīrtvā mukundo pañcālānatha matsyāṃśca śakraprasthamathāgamat
translation: Later on, after crossing the river Dṛṣadvatī (Mod. Ghaggar) and the Sarasvatī, he passed through the Pāñcāla country and the Matsya[5] region and arrived at Indraprastha.
purport:
Footnote [5]:
The territory of Jaipur including Alwar and some part of Bharatpur—GDAMI, p. 128.

devanagari: तमुपागतमाकर्ण्य प्रीतो दुर्दर्शनं नृणाम् । अजातशत्रुर्निरगात् सोपाध्यायः सुहृद्वृतः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: tamupāgatamākarṇya prīto durdarśanaṃ nṛṇām ajātaśatrurniragāt sopādhyāyaḥ suhṛdvṛtaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ prī class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ prī class 9 verb]
[imperfect active third single], [aorist active third single]
[nominative single from √ vṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]

translation: Hearing the report of the arrival of Śrī Kṛṣṇa whose sight is very rare to ordinary human beings, king Yudhiṣṭhira who was friendly to all, was highly delighted and went out of the city (to receive him) along with his family priests and accompanied with his friends and well-wishers.

devanagari: गीतवादित्रघोषेण ब्रह्मघोषेण भूयसा । अभ्ययात् स हृषीकेशं प्राणाः प्राणमिवादृतः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: gītavāditraghoṣeṇa brahmaghoṣeṇa bhūyasā abhyayāt sa hṛṣīkeśaṃ prāṇāḥ prāṇamivādṛtaḥ
translation: In the symphonic combination of vocal music (glorifying Kṛṣṇa in songs) and instrumental music and with the loud chanting of the Vedas, he eagerly approached Lord Kṛṣṇa just as senses do (i.e. are revived instantaneously) after the return of the chief Prāṇa (vital breath).

devanagari: दृष्ट्वा विक्लिन्नहृदयः कृष्णं स्नेहेन पाण्डवः । चिराद् दृष्टं प्रियतमं सस्वजेऽथ पुनः पुनः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: dṛṣṭvā viklinnahṛdayaḥ kṛṣṇaṃ snehena pāṇḍavaḥ cirād dṛṣṭaṃ priyatamaṃ sasvaje

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ dṛś class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ dṛś class 1 verb]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Having seen Śrī Kṛṣṇa, his dearest friend and relative after a long time, the heart of Pāṇḍu’s son (Yudhiṣṭhira) melted with deep affection and he embraced him again and again.

devanagari: दोर्भ्यां परिष्वज्य रमामलालयं । मुकुन्दगात्रं नृपतिर्हताशुभः । लेभे परां निर्वृतिमश्रुलोचनो । हृष्यत्तनुर्विस्मृत लोकविभ्रमः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: dorbhyāṃ pariṣvajya ramāmalālayaṃ mukundagātraṃ nṛpatirhatāśubhaḥ lebhe parāṃ nirvṛtimaśrulocano hṛṣyattanurvismṛta lokavibhramaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ han class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ han class 2 verb]
[vocative single from √ han class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ han class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ han class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ han class 2 verb]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[nominative single from √ hṛṣ class 4 verb], [vocative single from √ hṛṣ class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ hṛṣ class 4 verb]

translation: Embracing with both of his hands the Person of Lord Kṛṣṇa, the sacred abode of the goddess Lakṣmī, the king Yudhiṣṭhira was purged of all sins. With his eyes overflowing with tears and the hair of his body standing on ends, he felt highly blessed and forgot all the delusions about the world.

devanagari: तं मातुलेयं परिरभ्य निर्वृतो । भीमः स्मयन् प्रेमजलाकुलेन्द्रियः । यमौ किरीटी च सुहृत्तमं मुदा । प्रवृद्धबाष्पाः परिरेभिरेऽच्युतम् ॥ २७...
english_devnagari: taṃ mātuleyaṃ parirabhya nirvṛto bhīmaḥ smayan premajalākulendriyaḥ yamau kirīṭī ca suhṛttamaṃ mudā pravṛddhabāṣpāḥ parirebhire

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Beaming with a smile and with all his senses and mind overwhelmed with the force of (overpowering) affection, Bhīma embraced his maternal cousin (son of his maternal uncle) and enjoyed great felicity. Arjuna and the twins Nakula and Sahadeva, with profuse tears of joy embraced Acyuta[6], the best of their friend and well-wisher.
purport:
Footnote [6]:
SR. explains: While Arjuna bent to bow Kṛṣṇa, being equal in age, Kṛṣṇa held him up in his embrace while he was bowed down by Nakula and Sahadeva, his juniors.

devanagari: अर्जुनेन परिष्वक्तो यमाभ्यामभिवादितः । ब्राह्मणेभ्यो नमस्कृत्य वृद्धेभ्यश्च यथार्हतः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: arjunena pariṣvakto yamābhyāmabhivāditaḥ brāhmaṇebhyo namaskṛtya vṛddhebhyaśca yathārhataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [dative plural from √ vṛdh class 1 verb], [ablative plural from √ vṛdh class 1 verb]
[dative plural from √ vṛdh class 1 verb], [ablative plural from √ vṛdh class 1 verb]

translation: Embraced by Arjuna and paid due respects by Nakula and Sahadeva, Kṛṣṇa saluted Brāhmaṇas and other elderly persons according to their seniority and other worth.

devanagari: मानिनो मानयामास कुरुसृञ्जयकैकयान् । सूतमागधगन्धर्वा वन्दिनश्चोपमंत्रिणः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: mānino mānayāmāsa kurusṛñjayakaikayān sūtamāgadhagandharvā vandinaścopamaṃtriṇaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ su class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ su class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ su class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ su class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ su class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ su class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ sū class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ sū class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ sū class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ sū class 2 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: Paid respects by them, Kṛṣṇa reciprocally honoured Kauravas, Sṛñjayas, Kekayas, bards like Sūta and Māgadha and celestial singers, bards and court-jesters[7] praised him while others played upon musical instruments, like tabors, conchs, lutes, drums and horns or trumpets, danced and sang.
purport:
Footnote [7]:
The annotators differ slightly in assigning these musical activities among sūtas—upto Brāhmaṇas and court-jesters. VT. is uncertain, hence Bhāgavata Candrikā is followed.

devanagari: मृदङ्गशङ्खपटह वीणापणवगोमुखैः । ब्राह्मणाश्चारविन्दाक्षं तुष्टुवुर्ननृतुर्जगुः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: mṛdaṅgaśaṅkhapaṭaha vīṇāpaṇavagomukhaiḥ brāhmaṇāścāravindākṣaṃ tuṣṭuvurnanṛturjaguḥ
translation: Paid respects by them, Kṛṣṇa reciprocally honoured Kauravas, Sṛñjayas, Kekayas, bards like Sūta and Māgadha and celestial singers, bards and court-jesters[7] praised him while others played upon musical instruments, like tabors, conchs, lutes, drums and horns or trumpets, danced and sang.
purport:
Footnote [7]:
The annotators differ slightly in assigning these musical activities among sūtas—upto Brāhmaṇas and court-jesters. VT. is uncertain, hence Bhāgavata Candrikā is followed.

devanagari: एवं सुहृद्भिः पर्यस्तः पुण्यश्लोकशिखामणिः । संस्तूयमानो भगवान् विवेशालङ्कृतं पुरम् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ suhṛdbhiḥ paryastaḥ puṇyaślokaśikhāmaṇiḥ saṃstūyamāno bhagavān viveśālaṅkṛtaṃ puram

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Lord Kṛṣṇa, the crest-jewel among persons with hallowing renown, was thus accompanied by friends and well- wishers who were eulogizing him. With them he entered the well decorated city of Indraprastha.

devanagari: संसिक्तवर्त्म करिणां मदगन्धतोयैः । चित्रध्वजैः कनकतोरणपूर्णकुम्भैः । मृष्टात्मभिर्नवदुकूलविभूषणस्रग् । गन्धैर्नृभिर्युवतिभिश्च विराजमा...
english_devnagari: saṃsiktavartma kariṇāṃ madagandhatoyaiḥ citradhvajaiḥ kanakatoraṇapūrṇakumbhaiḥ mṛṣṭātmabhirnavadukūlavibhūṣaṇasrag gandhairnṛbhiryuvatibhiśca virājamānam
translation: The roads of the city were sprinkled with scented water and the ichor that exuded from the temples of elephants in rut; it was decorated with variegated flags, golden arches and pitchers full of water (at the entrance of houses); it appeared beautiful with its well-washed citizens wearing new silken garments, ornaments, wreaths of flowers and perfumes as well as with young-women who were equally well-dressed, adorned and anointed in pigments.

devanagari: उद्दीप्तदीपबलिभिः प्रतिसद्म जाल । निर्यातधूपरुचिरं विलसत्पताकम् । मूर्धन्यहेमकलशै रजतोरुशृङ्गैः । जुष्टं ददर्श भवनैः कुरुराजधाम ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: uddīptadīpabalibhiḥ pratisadma jāla niryātadhūparuciraṃ vilasatpatākam mūrdhanyahemakalaśai rajatoruśṛṅgaiḥ juṣṭaṃ dadarśa bhavanaiḥ kururājadhāma

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [genitive dual from √ raj class 1 verb], [locative dual from √ raj class 1 verb]
[genitive dual from √ raj class 1 verb], [locative dual from √ raj class 1 verb]
[genitive dual from √ rañj class 1 verb], [locative dual from √ rañj class 1 verb]
[genitive dual from √ rañj class 1 verb], [locative dual from √ rañj class 1 verb]
[accusative single from √ juṣ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ juṣ class 3 verb], [accusative single from √ juṣ class 6 verb]

[nominative single from √ juṣ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ juṣ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ juṣ class 3 verb], [accusative single from √ juṣ class 3 verb], [nominative single from √ juṣ class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ juṣ class 6 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Kṛṣṇa saw the capital of the king of Kurus (Yudhi- ṣṭhira) teeming with houses that were illuminated with a row of big lights, offerings of flowers, etc. It was charming on account of the latticed windows out of which were issuing out scented fumes, and flags were merrily flapping on the housetops which were decorated with domes of gold pitchers surrounded with big crests of silver.

devanagari: प्राप्तं निशम्य नरलोचनपानपात्रम् । औत्सुक्यविश्लथितकेश दुकूलबन्धाः । सद्यो विसृज्य गृहकर्म पतींश्च तल्पे । द्रष्टुं ययुर्युवतयः स्म नर...
english_devnagari: prāptaṃ niśamya naralocanapānapātram autsukyaviślathitakeśa dukūlabandhāḥ sadyo visṛjya gṛhakarma patīṃśca talpe draṣṭuṃ yayuryuvatayaḥ sma narendramārge
translation: As soon as the young damsels heard of his arrival, they immediately abandoned their domestic work and even their husbands in bed and flocked to the royal road (the main street) to see the one object worthy of drinking with (the goblets in the form of) eyes by the people, even though due to their eagerness to see him and in their haste, their locks of hair got dishevelled and the knots of silken garments, loose.

devanagari: तस्मिन् सुसङ्कुल इभाश्वरथद्विपद्भिः । कृष्णंसभार्यमुपलभ्य गृहाधिरूढाः । नार्यो विकीर्य कुसुमैर्मनसोपगुह्य । सुस्वागतं विदधुरुत्स्मयवीक...
english_devnagari: tasmin susaṅkula ibhāśvarathadvipadbhiḥ kṛṣṇaṃsabhāryamupalabhya gṛhādhirūḍhāḥ nāryo vikīrya kusumairmanasopaguhya susvāgataṃ vidadhurutsmayavīkṣitena

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ īr class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ īr ]
[vocative single from √ īr class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ īr ]

translation: Women who climbed the terraces of their houses saw Kṛṣṇa along with his queens on the road that was crowded with elephants, horses, chariots and footmen. They showered flowers over him and embraced him with their hearts and extended a warm welcome to him with their profusely smiling looks.

devanagari: ऊचुः स्त्रियः पथि निरीक्ष्य मुकुन्दपत्‍नीः । तारा यथोडुपसहाः किमकार्यमूभिः । यच्चक्षुषां पुरुषमौलिरुदारहास । लीलावलोककलयोत्सवमातनोति ॥...
english_devnagari: ūcuḥ striyaḥ pathi nirīkṣya mukundapat‍nīḥ tārā yathoḍupasahāḥ kimakāryamūbhiḥ yaccakṣuṣāṃ puruṣamaulirudārahāsa līlāvalokakalayotsavamātanoti

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: Observing the queens of Mukunda (Kṛṣṇa) on the road accompanying their husband like constellations of stars surrounding the moon, the women of the town remarked to each other: (We don’t know) what meritorious act must have been done by these consorts of Kṛṣṇa whereby this excellent-most personality (of Kṛṣṇa) extends (offers) delight to their eyes with his noble smiles and winsome amorous looks.

devanagari: तत्र तत्रोपसङ्गम्य पौरा मङ्गलपाणयः । चक्रुः सपर्यां कृष्णाय श्रेणीमुख्या हतैनसः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: tatra tatropasaṅgamya paurā maṅgalapāṇayaḥ cakruḥ saparyāṃ kṛṣṇāya śreṇīmukhyā hatainasaḥ
translation: In the town, at every stage, citizens with auspicious things in their hands, approached Kṛṣṇa and worshipped him. So did the leaders of trade-guilds rid themselves of all sins.

devanagari: अन्तःपुरजनैः प्रीत्या मुकुन्दः फुल्ललोचनैः । ससम्भ्रमैरभ्युपेतः प्राविशद् राजमन्दिरम् ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: antaḥpurajanaiḥ prītyā mukundaḥ phullalocanaiḥ sasambhramairabhyupetaḥ prāviśad rājamandiram
translation: Warmly received by ladies of the harem with their eyes blooming with joy and excited with eagerness, Kṛṣṇa entered the palace of the king Yudhiṣṭhira.

devanagari: पृथा विलोक्य भ्रात्रेयं कृष्णं त्रिभुवनेश्वरम् । प्रीतात्मोत्थाय पर्यङ्कात् सस्नुषा परिषस्वजे ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: pṛthā vilokya bhrātreyaṃ kṛṣṇaṃ tribhuvaneśvaram prītātmotthāya paryaṅkāt sasnuṣā pariṣasvaje
translation: When Kuntī beheld her brother’s son Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the Lord of the three worlds, her heart was full of delight and affection. She got up from the couch along with her daughter- in-law Draupadī and hugged him (affectionately).

devanagari: गोविन्दं गृहमानीय देवदेवेशमादृतः । पूजायां नाविदत् कृत्यं प्रमोदोपहतो नृपः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: govindaṃ gṛhamānīya devadeveśamādṛtaḥ pūjāyāṃ nāvidat kṛtyaṃ pramodopahato nṛpaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

[nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: Having brought Kṛṣṇa, the God of gods to his palace, with respect and devotion, king Yudhiṣṭhira was so beside himself with joy that he could not understand how to proceed with, the formalities of worship (of such a respectable guest).

devanagari: पितृस्वसुर्गुरुस्त्रीणां कृष्णश्चक्रेऽभिवादनम् । स्वयं च कृष्णया राजन् भगिन्या चाभिवन्दितः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: pitṛsvasurgurustrīṇāṃ kṛṣṇaścakre svayaṃ ca kṛṣṇayā rājan bhaginyā cābhivanditaḥ

[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: O king Parīkṣit! Śrī Kṛṣṇa, however, bowed down to his paternal aunt Kuntī and paid respects to elderly ladies. He himself was bowed down by Draupadī and his sister Subhadrā.

devanagari: श्वश्र्वा सञ्चोदिता कृष्णा कृष्णपत्‍नीश्च सर्वशः । आनर्च रुक्मिणीं सत्यां भद्रां जाम्बवतीं तथा ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: śvaśrvā sañcoditā kṛṣṇā kṛṣṇapat‍nīśca sarvaśaḥ ānarca rukmiṇīṃ satyāṃ bhadrāṃ jāmbavatīṃ tathā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Instructed by her mother-in-law Kuntī, Draupadī duly worshipped the queens of Kṛṣṇa such as Rukmiṇī, Satyabhāmā, Bhadrā, Jāmbavatī, Kālindī, Mitravindā. Śaibyā, and the pious Nāgnajitī and other women-guests, with garments, wreaths of flowers, ornaments, etc.

devanagari: कालिन्दीं मित्रविन्दां च शैब्यां नाग्नजितीं सतीम् । अन्याश्चाभ्यागता यास्तु वासःस्रङ्मण्डनादिभिः ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: kālindīṃ mitravindāṃ ca śaibyāṃ nāgnajitīṃ satīm anyāścābhyāgatā yāstu vāsaḥsraṅmaṇḍanādibhiḥ
translation: Instructed by her mother-in-law Kuntī, Draupadī duly worshipped the queens of Kṛṣṇa such as Rukmiṇī, Satyabhāmā, Bhadrā, Jāmbavatī, Kālindī, Mitravindā. Śaibyā, and the pious Nāgnajitī and other women-guests, with garments, wreaths of flowers, ornaments, etc.

devanagari: सुखं निवासयामास धर्मराजो जनार्दनम् । ससैन्यं सानुगामत्यं सभार्यं च नवं नवम् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: sukhaṃ nivāsayāmāsa dharmarājo janārdanam sasainyaṃ sānugāmatyaṃ sabhāryaṃ ca navaṃ navam
translation: King Dharmarāja made comfortable arrangement of lodging for Śrī Kṛṣṇa and that of his queens, members of the army, followers and counsellors providing everyday with new objects of enjoyment.

devanagari: तर्पयित्वा खाण्डवेन वह्निं फाल्गुनसंयुतः । मोचयित्वा मयं येन राज्ञे दिव्या सभा कृता ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: tarpayitvā khāṇḍavena vahniṃ phālgunasaṃyutaḥ mocayitvā mayaṃ yena rājñe divyā sabhā kṛtā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: Accompanied with Arjuna, Kṛṣṇa propitiated the Fire-god, offering him the Khāṇḍava forest for consumption. He rescued Maya (from the fire) who constructed a heavenly assembly-hall for king Yudhiṣṭhira.

devanagari: उवास कतिचिन् मासान् राज्ञः प्रियचिकीर्षया । विहरन् रथमारुह्य फाल्गुनेन भटैर्वृतः ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: uvāsa katicin māsān rājñaḥ priyacikīrṣayā viharan rathamāruhya phālgunena bhaṭairvṛtaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ vṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]

translation:
With the desire of pleasing king Yudhiṣṭhira, Kṛṣṇa stayed many months at Inḍraprastha. Occasionally, surrounded by warriors, he rode out in a chariot with Arjuna for excursion and sport.
[back to top]

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्‍भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां । संहितायां दशमस्कन्धे उत्तरार्धे । कृष्णस्य इंद्रप्रस्थगमनं नाम एकसप्ततितमोऽध्यायः ॥ ७१ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmad‍bhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃ saṃhitāyāṃ daśamaskandhe uttarārdhe kṛṣṇasya iṃdraprasthagamanaṃ nāma ekasaptatitamo
